serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 200 g (one bowl or plate), representing a typical single-person salad portion with three toppings and no sauce as commonly served in Singapore food courts and casual dining establishments.

nutrient_source.title: Salad (3 toppings), no sauce — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Salad (3 toppings), no sauce' (cr_id F08010042). All per-100g values are sourced at confidence 0.95 as recorded in the input metadata.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a customisable 3-topping undressed salad. GI estimated at 32 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for mixed green salad / raw mixed vegetables (GI range 15–38, representative value 32). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (32) × available carbohydrate per serving (2.95 g/100g × 200 g serving = 5.9 g) / 100 = 1.9. This is consistent with the very low carbohydrate content of the dish.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium at 129 mg/100g with vegetable-derived potassium supporting a favourable Na:K ratio
hypertension.guidance: At 129 mg sodium per 100 g, this undressed salad is well within the DASH diet sodium target; patients with hypertension may consume it freely, provided topping selections avoid processed meats, pickled vegetables, or cured fish that would substantially increase sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (2.95 g/100g), low GI (~32), dietary fibre 1.7 g/100g, and absence of dressing minimise postprandial glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycaemic load of approximately 1.9 per 200 g serving and a low GI of ~32, this undressed salad has a negligible impact on blood glucose and is an excellent choice for individuals managing type 2 diabetes; starchy toppings such as croutons, corn, or potato should be avoided to preserve the low glycaemic profile.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low total fat (0.53 g/100g), low energy density (23 kcal/100g), and high vegetable content supporting heart-healthy dietary patterns
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The extremely low fat and energy density of this undressed salad aligns with AHA dietary guidelines for cardiovascular risk reduction; patients should select toppings low in saturated fat and avoid processed meat toppings to maintain the cardiovascular benefit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very low fat, no pro-inflammatory preparation, antioxidant-rich vegetable base
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The low-fat, undressed vegetable base provides antioxidants and fibre that support an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should monitor tolerance to nightshade toppings (tomato, bell pepper) and may enhance benefit by selecting anti-inflammatory toppings such as walnuts or flaxseeds.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salad (3 toppings), no sauce is a customisable mixed fresh vegetable salad served without any dressing or sauce, with the diner's choice of three toppings. As recorded in the Singapore HPB Foodo database (cr_id F08010042), the dish provides only 23 kcal per 100 g, making it one of the lowest-energy-density options available in Singapore food courts and casual dining settings. The base consists of mixed salad leaves and fresh vegetables; the three toppings are variable and may include items such as boiled egg, tuna, corn, cucumber, tomato, or other common salad additions. The absence of dressing is nutritionally significant: it eliminates the primary source of fat, sugar, and sodium that would otherwise dominate the macronutrient profile. Per 100 g, the dish contains 1.55 g protein, 0.53 g fat, 2.95 g carbohydrate, 2.6 g sugar, 1.7 g dietary fibre, and 129 mg sodium. Calcium is 35 mg and iron is 0.69 mg per 100 g. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 32 (low), yielding a glycaemic load of roughly 1.9 per standard 200 g serving. Clinically, this dish is suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease, provided topping choices are made thoughtfully to avoid high-sodium or high-saturated-fat additions. The dish is vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free in its base form, though allergen status and dietary suitability may change depending on topping selection. The very low carbohydrate content (2.95 g/100g) is consistent with ketogenic dietary patterns.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08010042) at confidence 0.95, representing an exact match to the named food item. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for mixed raw vegetables, which is the closest defensible approved-source equivalent.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available from HPB source): saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These detailed fatty acid, micronutrient, and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this customisable salad item (F08010042) as of 2026. Calcium (35 mg/100g) and iron (0.69 mg/100g) are reported by HPB and included. GI is an analogous estimate (~32) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for mixed raw vegetables; no direct GI measurement for this specific customisable preparation exists in approved sources. Dietary flags (vegetarian, vegan, halal, gluten-free, dairy-free) apply to the base salad only; allergen status and dietary suitability may change depending on topping selection. The 'keto' dietary tag from the input has been noted but not formally included in dietary_flags as it is not a standard Veritas flag; the very low carbohydrate content (2.95 g/100g) is consistent with ketogenic dietary patterns. No zero-value core macros are present in this record.
